name: Vitamin D Status (Population-Level)
definition: Vitamin D status is commonly measured via serum 25(OH)D and is studied as a population-level modifier of immune-related outcomes, including multiple sclerosis risk in some epidemiological contexts. GENARCH treats vitamin D status as an educational exposure/biomarker-linked category and does not provide supplementation advice.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Vitamin D signaling is linked to immune regulation; causal interpretation varies by study design and confounding.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: MS epidemiology often discusses earlier-life immune exposures and vitamin D proxies; interpretation remains population-level.

references.title: Vitamin D and multiple sclerosis risk: evidence overview (review)
